regex: System\.NullReferenceException.*Object reference not set to an instance of an object
title: NullReferenceException: Object reference not set
fixes: Add null check before accessing the object: if (obj != null) { ... }
Use null-conditional operator: obj?.Property
Use null-coalescing operator to provide default: obj ?? new Object()
Enable nullable reference types in .csproj: <Nullable>enable</Nullable>

regex: InvalidOperationException.*Sequence contains no elements
title: InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains no elements
fixes: Use FirstOrDefault() instead of First()
Use SingleOrDefault() instead of Single()
Check Any() before calling First()/Single()
Provide a default with DefaultIfEmpty() before aggregation

regex: InvalidOperationException.*Collection was modified.*enumeration
title: InvalidOperationException: Collection modified during enumeration
fixes: Call .ToList() before iterating: foreach (var item in list.ToList())
Use a for loop with reverse index when removing items
Use ConcurrentDictionary/ConcurrentBag for thread-safe scenarios
Collect items to remove in a separate list, then remove after iteration

regex: System\.ArgumentNullException.*Value cannot be null.*Parameter name: (\w+)
title: ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null
fixes: Ensure the argument is not null before passing it to the method
Add a guard clause: ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(param)
Check DI registration — the service may not be registered in the container
Verify configuration/appsettings values are not missing

regex: System\.ArgumentException.*is not a valid value
title: ArgumentException: Invalid argument value
fixes: Validate input before passing to method
Check enum values with Enum.IsDefined() before casting
Ensure string arguments are not empty: ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrEmpty(param)

regex: System\.InvalidCastException.*Unable to cast object of type '(.+)' to type '(.+)'
title: InvalidCastException: Unable to cast object
fixes: Use 'as' operator with null check instead of direct cast: var x = obj as TargetType
Use pattern matching: if (obj is TargetType typed) { ... }
Verify the actual runtime type with GetType() before casting
Use Convert.ChangeType() for value type conversions

regex: error CS0103.*The name '(.+)' does not exist in the current context
title: CS0103: Name does not exist in current context
fixes: Add the missing using directive for the namespace
Check for typos in the variable/method name
Ensure the variable is declared in the correct scope
Add a project reference if the symbol is in another assembly

regex: error CS0234.*The type or namespace name '(.+)' does not exist in the namespace '(.+)'
title: CS0234: Type or namespace not found
fixes: Install the missing NuGet package: dotnet add package <PackageName>
Add a project reference: dotnet add reference <ProjectPath>
Check the target framework — the type may not be available in your TFM
Verify the assembly is not conditionally excluded in .csproj

regex: error CS1061.*'(.+)' does not contain a definition for '(.+)'
title: CS1061: Type does not contain definition for member
fixes: Check for typos in the member name
Add the missing using directive for extension methods
Verify the NuGet package version — the member may have been added/removed
Cast to the correct type if using an interface reference

regex: error CS0029.*Cannot implicitly convert type '(.+)' to '(.+)'
title: CS0029: Cannot implicitly convert type
fixes: Add an explicit cast: (TargetType)value
Use a conversion method: int.Parse(), ToString(), Convert.ToInt32()
Fix the return type of the method to match the actual returned value
Add an implicit/explicit operator overload if you own the types

regex: warning CS8600.*Converting null literal or possible null value to non-nullable type
title: CS8600: Possible null to non-nullable conversion
fixes: Declare the variable as nullable: string? result = ...
Add a null check or guard before assignment
Use the null-forgiving operator if you're certain it's not null: value!
Use pattern matching: if (value is string s) { ... }

regex: warning CS8602.*Dereference of a possibly null reference
title: CS8602: Dereference of possibly null reference
fixes: Add a null check before dereferencing: if (obj != null) obj.Method()
Use null-conditional operator: obj?.Method()
Add [NotNull] attribute if analysis is wrong
Use the null-forgiving operator if certain: obj!.Method()

regex: warning CS8604.*Possible null reference argument for parameter
title: CS8604: Possible null reference argument
fixes: Add a null check before passing the argument
Use the ?? operator to provide a fallback: param ?? defaultValue
Mark the parameter as nullable in the method signature: string? param
Use the null-forgiving operator if you're certain: Method(value!)

regex: NETSDK1045.*The current \.NET SDK does not support targeting
title: NETSDK1045: SDK does not support target framework
fixes: Install the required .NET SDK version from https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download
Update global.json to use an installed SDK version
Lower the TargetFramework in .csproj to match your installed SDK
Run 'dotnet --list-sdks' to check installed versions

regex: NETSDK1005.*Project .+ targets '(.+)'.*current SDK
title: NETSDK1005: Target framework version mismatch
fixes: Install the matching .NET SDK for the target framework
Change TargetFramework in .csproj to match installed SDK
Delete global.json if it pins an unavailable SDK version
Use 'dotnet --info' to verify the active SDK

regex: No migrations were applied.*database is already up to date|The migration '(.+)' has already been applied
title: EF Core: Migration already applied or database up to date
fixes: Run 'dotnet ef migrations list' to check applied migrations
Create a new migration: dotnet ef migrations add <Name>
If schema is out of sync, remove last migration and re-add
Check __EFMigrationsHistory table in the database

regex: Cannot open database|A network-related or instance-specific error|Login failed for user
title: EF Core / SQL: Connection string or database access error
fixes: Verify ConnectionStrings section in appsettings.json
Ensure the database server is running and accessible
Check that the user credentials in the connection string are correct
For LocalDB: run 'sqllocaldb start MSSQLLocalDB'

regex: InvalidOperationException.*No service for type '(.+)' has been registered|Unable to resolve service for type '(.+)'
title: ASP.NET Core DI: Service not registered
fixes: Register the service in Program.cs: builder.Services.AddScoped<IService, Service>()
Check that the interface and implementation match the registered types
Ensure the service is registered before Build() is called
For generic types, use AddScoped(typeof(IRepo<>), typeof(Repo<>))

regex: Access to fetch.*blocked by CORS|has been blocked by CORS policy|No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin'
title: ASP.NET Core: CORS policy blocking requests
fixes: Add CORS in Program.cs: builder.Services.AddCors() with a policy allowing the origin
Call app.UseCors("policyName") BEFORE app.UseAuthorization()
Ensure the allowed origins match exactly (include scheme and port)
For development, use .AllowAnyOrigin().AllowAnyMethod().AllowAnyHeader()

regex: StatusCode.*404|NotFound|No route matches|endpoint routing.*did not match
title: ASP.NET Core: 404 Not Found / Route not matched
fixes: Verify the [Route] and [Http*] attributes on the controller/action
Ensure app.MapControllers() is called in Program.cs
Check that the controller class has [ApiController] attribute
Verify route parameters match method parameter names exactly

regex: The SSL connection could not be established|AuthenticationException.*remote certificate
title: HttpClient: SSL/TLS certificate error
fixes: For development, bypass with HttpClientHandler.ServerCertificateCustomValidationCallback
Install/trust the development certificate: dotnet dev-certs https --trust
Ensure the server certificate is valid and not expired
Update System.Net.Http and target a modern TLS version

regex: TaskCanceledException.*A task was canceled|HttpRequestException.*request timed out|The request was canceled due to.*timeout
title: HttpClient: Request timeout
fixes: Increase HttpClient.Timeout: client.Timeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(120)
Use CancellationTokenSource with a longer timeout
Check if the server is responding — test with curl/Postman
Use IHttpClientFactory with named/typed clients and configure timeout per client

regex: HttpRequestException.*Connection refused|No connection could be made|actively refused
title: HttpClient: Connection refused
fixes: Verify the target service is running on the expected host:port
Check the base URL in configuration (appsettings/environment variable)
Ensure firewall/network allows the connection
For Docker: use the container name or host.docker.internal instead of localhost

regex: JsonException.*cycle.*detected|A possible object cycle was detected
title: JSON Serialization: Object cycle detected
fixes: Add ReferenceHandler.IgnoreCycles: options.ReferenceHandler = ReferenceHandler.IgnoreCycles
Use [JsonIgnore] on navigation properties that cause circular references
Create DTOs without circular references instead of serializing entities directly
Configure globally in Program.cs: builder.Services.AddControllers().AddJsonOptions(...)

regex: JsonException.*could not be converted|Cannot deserialize|The JSON value could not be converted to
title: JSON Deserialization: Type conversion error
fixes: Check that JSON property types match C# model types (e.g., string vs int)
Add JsonNumberHandling.AllowReadingFromString for numeric strings
Use [JsonConverter] for custom type mapping
Ensure property names match (use [JsonPropertyName] or PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true)

regex: error MSB4019.*The imported project.*was not found
title: MSB4019: Imported project not found
fixes: Restore NuGet packages: dotnet restore
Install the required workload: dotnet workload install <workload>
Check that the .NET SDK/targeting pack is installed
Delete obj/ and bin/ folders and rebuild

regex: error NU1101.*Unable to find package|NU1102.*Unable to find package.*in any source
title: NU1101/NU1102: NuGet package not found
fixes: Check the package name for typos
Add the correct NuGet source in nuget.config
Clear NuGet cache: dotnet nuget locals all --clear
Check if the package version exists: dotnet package search <name>

regex: InvalidOperationException.*DbContext.*disposed|Cannot access a disposed context|disposed object.*DbContext
title: EF Core: DbContext already disposed
fixes: Don't capture DbContext in a background task — inject IDbContextFactory<T> instead
Register DbContext as Scoped (default), not Transient or Singleton
Avoid using DbContext after the request scope ends (e.g., in fire-and-forget tasks)
Use IServiceScopeFactory to create a new scope for background work

regex: InvalidOperationException.*Middleware.*must be added before|UseRouting.*must be added before UseEndpoints|UseAuthentication.*must be called before UseAuthorization
title: ASP.NET Core: Middleware order incorrect
fixes: Follow the correct middleware order: UseRouting → UseAuthentication → UseAuthorization → MapControllers
Place UseCors() before UseAuthorization()
Place UseHttpsRedirection() early in the pipeline
Refer to ASP.NET Core middleware documentation for canonical ordering

regex: error MSB3027.*Could not copy.*being used by another process|IOException.*used by another process
title: MSB3027: File locked by another process during build
fixes: Stop the running application (dotnet run / IIS Express / kestrel)
Close any file handles — check with Process Explorer or 'handle.exe'
Delete bin/ and obj/ folders and rebuild
Disable 'Build on Save' if using an IDE extension that triggers concurrent builds
